tailwinds.relevance: Active GSE re-fleeting and electrification budget at a SAW handler is a natural insertion point for retrofit autonomy.

tailwinds.headline: Sabiha Gokcen unveils renderings of a new passenger terminal adding ~4M annual pax capacity, eight gates, 240 m connector
tailwinds.relevance: Terminal expansion signals sustained growth and new airside infrastructure planning where automation can be designed in.

operationsNotes: Mixed GSE model: Pegasus (roughly the anchor LCC at SAW) self-handles ramp with its own GSE since 2016, while TGS and Celebi handle other carriers. Second runway (3,540 m, wide-body capable) plus Terminal 1 renovation added ~5.5M annual pax capacity; airport handled 270,000+ flights in 2025 with a Sept 7, 2025 daily record of 856 movements. High-tempo narrowbody turn environment suited to baggage-tractor autonomy.
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE vendor activity found at SAW or anywhere in Turkey (searches for TractEasy/EasyMile, Aurrigo, UISEE, AeroVect in Turkey returned nothing). Attractive profile: Europe's fastest-growing large hub (48M+ pax 2025, +17% YoY), second runway doubling movements, and three distinct GSE fleet owners as entry paths. Pegasus in-house ground ops is the single largest tractor fleet owner (carrier-led buyer); Celebi is actively re-fleeting to electric GSE (retrofit timing fit); TGS is the THY-affiliated handler. No known AeroVect engagement.

name: Minneapolis–Saint Paul International Airport / Wold–Chamberlain Field

groundHandlers.name: Delta Air Lines (self-handling)
groundHandlers.role: Mainline ramp/baggage at its MSP hub (Delta ~65% of traffic); Delta hires its own MSP ramp agents and GSE maintenance staff
groundHandlers.notes: Sources: https://delta.dejobs.org/locations/minneapolis-mn/jobs/ and https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Minneapolis%E2%80%93Saint_Paul_International_Airport

groundHandlers.name: Unifi Aviation
groundHandlers.role: Ramp handling under Delta full-handling contract at MSP (regional/overflow)
groundHandlers.notes: Active MSP ramp agent postings labeled 'MSP Delta Full Handling': https://unifi.avature.net/careers/JobDetail/1000-MSP-Delta-Full-Handling-RD2AGT-Ramp-Agent-PT/6339

groundHandlers.name: Swissport
groundHandlers.role: Ramp handling, passenger services, fueling and baggage services at MSP
groundHandlers.notes: Station page: https://www.swissport.com/en/network/north-america/united-states/msp (page confirmed via search; direct fetch blocked 403)

groundHandlers.name: GAT Ground Support / PrimeFlight
groundHandlers.role: Ramp and baggage handling at MSP; GAT acquired by PrimeFlight Feb 2026
groundHandlers.notes: GAT is AeroVect's US deployment partner. MSP presence: https://www.indeed.com/cmp/Gat-Airline-Ground-Support/salaries/Baggage-Handler/Minneapolis-Saint-Paul-MN and https://talents.vaia.com/companies/primeflight/ramp-agent-minneapolis-saint-paul-international-airport-msp-34647165/

groundHandlers.name: Worldwide Flight Services (WFS)
groundHandlers.role: Cargo and ramp services at MSP per local job listings
groundHandlers.notes: Surfaced in MSP ramp job listings: https://www.indeed.com/q-ramp-agent-l-minneapolis,-mn-jobs.html

tailwinds.headline: Delta electrifying GSE at MSP and other hubs: $385M invested in eGSE and charging 2020-2024, targeting 100% electric core GSE in 5 hubs by end of 2025
tailwinds.relevance: Fleet turnover to electric tractors and new charging infrastructure at MSP is a natural insertion point for retrofit autonomy on the same vehicles

tailwinds.headline: MSP Airport Modernization Program: $242M Delta-managed renovation of six Terminal 1 concourses and ~75 Delta gates, completing 2025 (MAC funding ~75%)
tailwinds.relevance: Shows active MAC-Delta capital partnership and modernization appetite at the airport where autonomy pilots would be approved

tailwinds.headline: PrimeFlight acquires GAT Airline Ground Support (~6,000 employees, ~70 North American airports)
tailwinds.relevance: AeroVect's US partner GAT is folding into PrimeFlight, which already runs ramp agents at MSP, creating a direct handler channel onto this ramp

operationsNotes: Delta and regional affiliates account for about 65% of MSP passenger traffic (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Minneapolis%E2%80%93Saint_Paul_International_Airport). GSE model is mixed: Delta carrier-led on mainline, with handler-led ramps via Unifi (Delta full handling), Swissport, GAT/PrimeFlight and WFS for other carriers and cargo. Labor signals are recurring but concentrated in service contractors rather than ramp crews: 1,000 SEIU Local 26 passenger-service workers (cabin cleaners, wheelchair agents at ERMC, Prospect, G2, Global Elite) campaigned for a $20 minimum as their contract expired in 2024 (https://sahanjournal.com/business-work/msp-airport-workers-demanding-increased-wages/); Twin Cities janitors and security guards, including MSP airport, prepared strikes in March 2025 (https://www.startribune.com/janitors-security-guard-prepare-strike-around-twin-cities-march-2-target-msp-airport-best-buy-us-bank-stadium/600346185); HMSHost food workers set a Thanksgiving 2025 strike deadline (https://www.mprnews.org/story/2025/11/18/msp-food-workers-strike-deadline-thanksgiving-rush) and won gains in December 2025 (https://advocate.stpaulunions.org/2025/12/12/hospitality-workers-at-msp-make-gains-after-holiday-strike-threat/). Third-party ramp wages run about $18/hr at Unifi vs $20.55/hr at Delta mainline per active postings.
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE vendor found deployed or trialing at MSP in multiple searches, so the ramp is open. Attractive entry points: Delta hub with heavy eGSE electrification spend, and AeroVect partner GAT (now PrimeFlight) already staffs ramp/baggage at MSP, giving an existing channel onto the field. Delta self-handles mainline, so carrier-level buy-in likely required for the largest share of turns.

name: Fort Lauderdale Hollywood International Airport

groundHandlers.name: Swissport
groundHandlers.role: Ramp handling, passenger services, fueling, baggage and cargo; at FLL since 1983
groundHandlers.notes: Source: https://www.swissport.com/en/network/north-america/united-states/fll

groundHandlers.name: Menzies Aviation
groundHandlers.role: Ground services (above and below wing) and into-plane fueling; handles wide-body and narrow-body
groundHandlers.notes: Source: https://menziesaviation.com/our-network/fort-lauderdale-fll/

groundHandlers.name: Unifi
groundHandlers.role: Ramp and cabin services; active FLL postings including cabin service for United
groundHandlers.notes: Source: https://www.ziprecruiter.com/c/Unifi/Job/Airport-Cabin-Service-Agent-FLL-UA/-in-Fort-Lauderdale,FL?jid=40f054181ad31c42

groundHandlers.name: Alliance Ground International (AGI)
groundHandlers.notes: Source: https://agi.aero/fort-lauderdale-fl-ground-handling-services/

groundHandlers.name: Eulen America / G2 Secure Staff
groundHandlers.role: Airline-contracted baggage, cabin and wheelchair services (historical presence per SEIU 32BJ)
groundHandlers.notes: Source: https://www.seiu32bj.org/press-release/fll-airport-workers-earning-poverty-wages-strike-over-bullying-anti-union-tactics/

tailwinds.headline: Terminal 5 under construction: ~$404M, 5 new gates, ~230,000 sq ft, completion mid-2026
tailwinds.relevance: New gates and apron activity expand ramp operations and equipment needs, a natural moment to spec new GSE workflows including autonomy

tailwinds.headline: JetBlue expands FLL: 11 new destinations, ~130 daily departures, 75%+ more flights than 2025
tailwinds.relevance: Largest JetBlue operation in its history at FLL requires rapid ramp capacity scale-up, favoring productivity tech like retrofit autonomous tractors

tailwinds.headline: Spirit shutdown leaves FLL terminal empty as JetBlue, Frontier, Breeze and Allegiant expand routes
tailwinds.relevance: 12 vacated Spirit gates are being redistributed; incoming carriers and their handlers must stand up ground operations quickly, opening new GSE contracts

operationsNotes: Mixed GSE model: several third-party handlers (Swissport since 1983, Menzies ground + fuel, Unifi, AGI) operate alongside large carrier-run operations; Spirit employed roughly 2,500 people at FLL, its largest base, before shutting down May 2, 2026. Labor picture is two-sided: chronic low-wage, high-turnover contractor workforce with a history of SEIU 32BJ strikes (Eulen America, G2 Secure Staff), and a May 2026 shock in which Spirit's shutdown cut 2,529 FLL jobs including ~200 contract ramp/cabin workers, creating short-term surplus but heavy re-staffing churn as replacement carriers expand.
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE vendor found on this ramp. Attractive window: Spirit's May 2026 collapse freed 12 gates and JetBlue, Frontier, Breeze and Allegiant are scaling FLL fast while Terminal 5 adds 5 gates mid-2026, so handlers and carriers must rebuild ramp capacity quickly. Multiple third-party handlers (Swissport, Menzies, Unifi, AGI) give AeroVect several entry points; GAT/PrimeFlight presence at FLL not confirmed.

name: El Dorado International Airport

groundHandlers.name: Talma Servicios Aeroportuarios
groundHandlers.notes: Acquired Avianca's handling subsidiary SAI in Feb 2023, taking over ramp services for hub carrier Avianca at BOG and ~20 Colombian airports; also acquired LASA in 2019. Opened a $4M, 7,400 sqm GSE maintenance center (CET) at El Dorado in May 2025 servicing pushbacks, GPUs, ASUs, ramp buses, belt loaders and stairs. Sources: https://www.infobae.com/america/agencias/2023/02/02/la-empresa-peruana-talma-anuncia-compra-de-la-colombiana-sai-a-avianca/ and https://forbes.co/2025/05/07/negocios/talma-invierte-us4-millones-en-un-centro-especializado-en-bogota

groundHandlers.name: Menzies Aviation
groundHandlers.role: Ground services and air cargo handler
groundHandlers.notes: Provides above and below wing ground services, cargo, and meet-and-assist for wide and narrow body aircraft at BOG. Source: https://menziesaviation.com/our-network/bogota-bog/

tailwinds.headline: EDMAX private-initiative PPP to expand El Dorado to 73M passengers/year with ~$2.5B investment; selection process advancing through 2026
tailwinds.relevance: Major capacity expansion including cargo terminal modernization and more parking positions increases ramp vehicle traffic and pressure to modernize GSE operations.

tailwinds.headline: Talma invests $4M in a 7,400 sqm GSE maintenance center at El Dorado plus $5M more in 2025 for new specialized equipment
tailwinds.relevance: The dominant handler is actively modernizing and expanding its GSE fleet and maintenance capability at BOG, exactly the buying motion an autonomy retrofit vendor can attach to.

tailwinds.headline: El Dorado invests $5.6M in modernization ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup
tailwinds.relevance: Near-term traffic surge and modernization budget create urgency for throughput and turnaround efficiency on the ramp.

operationsNotes: Avianca hub (Latin America's third-busiest airport by traffic). GSE model is handler-led: Avianca divested its in-house handler SAI to Talma in Feb 2023, so hub ramp handling sits with a third party (https://www.portafolio.co/negocios/empresas/servicios-aereos-integrados-empresa-peruana-talma-completa-la-compra-total-de-la-compania-que-pertenecia-a-avianca-577887). Labor signals: Migracion Colombia union strike caused mass delays and missed flights at El Dorado in Dec 2024 (https://www.eltiempo.com/politica/gobierno/migracion-colombia-y-sindicatos-llegan-a-acuerdo-para-levantar-huelga-en-aeropuertos-3406619); DIAN customs unions ran a plan tortuga blocking El Dorado access in Nov 2025 (https://www.infobae.com/colombia/2025/11/07/grupos-de-sindicatos-de-la-dian-bloquean-la-26-y-planean-plan-tortuga-hasta-aeropuerto-el-dorado/); Colombia's Law 2466 labor reform expands night hours to 7pm-6am with a 35% surcharge from Jan 2026 and raises Sunday/holiday pay to 100% by 2027, a structural cost increase for 24/7 ramp operations (https://www.littler.com/news-analysis/asap/colombia-recent-labor-reforms-bring-key-changes-and-new-compliance-priorities). Rated medium, not high, because no ramp-worker-specific strike was found.
aerovectNotes: No purpose-built autonomous-GSE vendor (TractEasy/EasyMile, Aurrigo, UISEE) found on the BOG ramp. Talma is the anchor target: post-SAI acquisition it runs the Avianca hub ramp plus ~20 Colombian stations, and is actively investing in GSE infrastructure at BOG ($4M maintenance center opened May 2025, additional $5M equipment spend announced June 2025), so a retrofit RaaS pitch scales across its network. Tailwinds: EDMAX $2.5B expansion to 73M pax and 2026 labor reform surcharges raising 24/7 ramp labor costs. Constraints: no Colombian airside autonomy precedent found, and the concession is in transition pending EDMAX award (Opain/Odinsa), which could slow procurement.

name: King Khalid International Airport

groundHandlers.name: Saudi Ground Services (SGS)
groundHandlers.role: Dominant ramp and passenger handler; won Riyadh Air's SAR 1.5B (USD 400M) 4.5-year ground handling contract (ramp + passenger terminal) starting 1 Sep 2024
groundHandlers.notes: Signed 14 Aug 2024 with option to extend 2 years; SGS also handles Saudia group at RUH. Sources: https://www.riyadhair.com/en/media-hub/riyadh-air-awards-sar-1-5-billion-contract--to-saudi-ground-serv and https://www.agbi.com/aviation/2024/08/riyadh-air-awards-400m-ground-handling-contract/

groundHandlers.name: Swissport Saudi Arabia
groundHandlers.role: Third-party handler at RUH since 2016; passenger services and ramp handling
groundHandlers.notes: Scaled to 13 Saudi airports from 1 Jun 2025. Sources: https://www.swissport.com/en/network/middle-east/saudi-arabia/ruh and https://www.swissport.com/en/news/current-news/2025/swissport-scales-up-in-saudi-arabia-with-groundbreaking-13-airport-launch

groundHandlers.name: SAL Saudi Logistics Services
groundHandlers.role: Cargo ground handler at RUH; signed yearly renewable cargo handling agreement with Singapore Airlines (Jul 2026)
groundHandlers.notes: Cargo-side only per source. Source: https://www.foodbusinessmea.com/sal-logistics-secures-singapore-airlines-ground-handling-contract-at-riyadhs-king-khalid-airport/

tailwinds.headline: King Salman International Airport breaks ground on third runway on the existing KKIA site; movements to rise from 65 to ~85 per hour, targeting 100M passengers by 2030
tailwinds.relevance: Massive multi-year capacity build-out on the RUH campus means longer tow routes, new tender cycles, and pressure to scale ramp throughput faster than headcount can grow, a strong fit for retrofit autonomy

tailwinds.headline: Riyadh Air awards SAR 1.5B (USD 400M) ground handling contract to SGS at RUH, 4.5 years from 1 Sep 2024
tailwinds.relevance: A brand-new flag carrier scaling from zero at RUH locks in SGS as the anchor handler with budget and incentive to modernize GSE for a high-growth operation

tailwinds.headline: Swissport scales up in Saudi Arabia with 13-airport network launch from 1 June 2025
tailwinds.relevance: Swissport, already piloting Aurrigo Auto-DollyTug at ZRH, is expanding Saudi ramp operations including Riyadh; a global-account channel that cuts both ways for AeroVect

operationsNotes: Handler-led GSE model: ramp handling at RUH is performed by licensed third-party handlers (SGS for Saudia group and Riyadh Air; Swissport for other carriers; SAL on cargo), not by carriers. Riyadh Airports deployed AWS/AIRIS AI predictive maintenance on KKIA baggage handling systems in Dec 2022 (claimed world first), signaling automation appetite (https://www.aci-asiapac.aero/media-centre/news/riyadh-airports-records-a-new-achievement-by-adding-artificial-intelligence-ai-technologies-to-enhance-baggage-handling-systems-at-king-khalid-international-airport-in-cooperation-with-amazon-aws-and-airis). Labor: Saudi aviation is described as one of the Kingdom's most talent-constrained sectors with passenger volumes set to roughly triple to 300M by 2030 (https://tascoutsourcing.sa/en/insights/reports/Saudi-Aviation-Industry), and new Saudization quotas for aviation professions carry work-permit penalties for non-compliance (https://www.fragomen.com/insights/saudi-arabia-new-saudization-requirements-for-select-professions-in-aviation-customer-service-centers-and-other-sectors.html). No strike activity found; industrial action is effectively restricted in Saudi Arabia, so pressure shows up as shortage and quota compliance rather than strikes.
aerovectNotes: Open ramp: no autonomous-GSE vendor found trialed or deployed at RUH. SGS is the anchor account (dominant national handler, USD 400M Riyadh Air ramp contract) and publicly leans into automation and electrification. Watch two channel dynamics: Swissport at RUH is a potential entry path but is Aurrigo's marquee partner at ZRH, and EasyMile/TractEasy already co-wrote Saudi AV regulations with the TGA, giving it a certification head start in-country. KSIA construction on the KKIA site creates new tender windows through 2030.

name: Narita International Airport

tailwinds.headline: Japan labour shortages prompt ground-handling recruitment drive; ~5% ramp-handling and ~14% passenger-handling workforce decline vs 2019, Narita denied added flights for lack of handlers
tailwinds.relevance: Documents structural ground-handling labor shortage in Japan (ramp handling down ~5% vs March 2019 across 61 major handlers), the underlying tailwind for GSE automation demand. (URL cited from search summary; direct fetch returned a bot-verification page.)

competitors.status: deployed (Level 4 autonomous baggage/cargo towing with JAL, Japan’s first, from Dec 15 2025)

aerovectNotes: TractEasy/EasyMile run Level 4 autonomous EZTow towing with JAL here (announced Dec 2025), the same carrier-led autonomy wave as Haneda. Purpose-built vendor entrenched; retrofit angle would ride JAL’s wider mixed fleet.

name: Taiwan Taoyuan International Airport

groundHandlers.name: Taoyuan International Airport Services (TIAS)
groundHandlers.role: Dominant ramp/baggage/cargo handler; handles 30 of 38 airlines at TPE, over 75% market share
groundHandlers.notes: JV of China Airlines (49%), Ministry of Transportation & Communications (45%), UPS (6%). Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taoyuan_International_Airport_Services_Limited

groundHandlers.name: Evergreen Airline Services Corp (EGAS)
groundHandlers.role: Second ramp handler; baggage, cargo, mail, aerobridge operations
groundHandlers.notes: Evergreen Group / EVA Air affiliate. Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taoyuan_International_Airport and https://www.taiwannews.com.tw/news/4769130

tailwinds.headline: Terminal 3 north concourse began trial operations with first passenger flights
tailwinds.relevance: Major capacity expansion adds ramp movements and new-build stands/infrastructure, increasing GSE demand at a two-handler airport already short on ramp labor.

tailwinds.headline: LILEE Systems appointed to deliver L3/L4 autonomous electric People Mover System for Terminal 3
tailwinds.relevance: Airport is already procuring Level 3/4 autonomous vehicles (landside shuttle, on top of the earlier ITRI L4 inter-terminal shuttle), showing institutional and regulatory acceptance of AVs on airport property.

tailwinds.headline: AI boom fuels cargo surge at Taoyuan Airport free trade zone; automation cutting entry-level handling roles
tailwinds.relevance: Record AI-chip air cargo volumes raise tug/dolly cycle demand exactly where labor is scarce, strengthening autonomy ROI for cargo tractor routes between terminals, cargo terminals, and the FTZ.

operationsNotes: Two-handler duopoly, both carrier-affiliated JVs (China Airlines side and Evergreen/EVA side), so GSE is handler-owned but airline-aligned. Separate cargo terminals run by Taiwan Air Cargo Terminals and Evergreen Air Cargo Services (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taoyuan_International_Airport). Roughly 2.35M tonnes of cargo in 2024, ~62% electronics/semiconductors, growing 6-8% on AI chip exports (https://kitalent.com/articles/article-taoyuan-air-cargo-talent-bifurcation/). Terminal 3 in phased opening since Dec 2025 (https://focustaiwan.tw/society/202512010012).
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE vendor found on the TPE ramp (searches for TractEasy/EasyMile, Aurrigo, UISEE and Chinese-language queries for autonomous baggage tractors returned nothing airside). Ramp is a two-handler market: entry runs through TIAS (China Airlines/MOTC/UPS JV, 75%+ share) or EGAS (EVA/Evergreen). Labor case is strong: EGAS ground crew staged a coordinated-leave strike over Lunar New Year 2023 delaying 79 flights (https://focustaiwan.tw/business/202301010013), TIAS narrowly averted its own strike (https://news.pts.org.tw/article/618427), and Taiwan's Ministry of Labor launched hire-then-train programs with both handlers because post-COVID ramp recruiting failed (https://vocus.cc/article/64c87c1ffd897800016b02c5); talent scarcity in the Taoyuan air cargo cluster persists into 2026 (https://kitalent.com/articles/article-taoyuan-air-cargo-talent-bifurcation/). AV precedent exists: ITRI L4 inter-terminal shuttle billed as world's second operational airport self-driving shuttle (https://itritoday.itri.org/113/content/en/unit_03-1.html) plus the LILEE T3 autonomous PMS. Retrofit pitch fits TIAS/EGAS-owned tractor fleets; T3 phased opening and AI-cargo growth are the timing hooks. Constraint to watch: state and carrier ownership of both handlers may slow procurement.

name: Antalya International Airport

groundHandlers.name: Havas Ground Handling
groundHandlers.role: Full-service ramp, passenger, cargo and warehouse handler (TAV subsidiary, Turkey's largest handler)
groundHandlers.notes: Provides ground handling plus warehouse services at Antalya per TAV and UN Logistics Cluster listings. Source: https://www.lca.logcluster.org/turkiye-223-antalya-international-airport and https://tavairports.com/about-us/services/havas

groundHandlers.name: Celebi Ground Handling (Celebi Hava Servisi)
groundHandlers.role: Full-service ramp handler at both domestic and international terminals, 24/7
groundHandlers.notes: Serves 32 airports in Turkiye including Antalya; buying electric GSE for Antalya under a March 2025 EBRD loan. Sources: https://www.celebiaviation.com/turkey and https://www.ebrd.com/home/news-and-events/news/2025/ebrd-supports-electrification-of-celebi-aviation-s-ground-handli.html

groundHandlers.name: TGS Turkish Ground Services
groundHandlers.role: Full-service handler (Turkish Airlines and Havas JV), one of 8 TGS stations; handles THY/AJet traffic
groundHandlers.notes: Operates at AYT with its own lost and found office listed on the airport site. Sources: https://www.antalya-airport.aero/passengers-visitors/passenger-guide/departing-from-ayt/lost-found/handling-lost-and-found-office/tgs-lost-and-found-office and https://www.lca.logcluster.org/turkiye-223-antalya-international-airport

groundHandlers.name: MNG Airlines
groundHandlers.notes: Listed as a cargo handler at AYT by the UN Logistics Cluster assessment. Source: https://www.lca.logcluster.org/turkiye-223-antalya-international-airport

tailwinds.headline: Antalya Airport expansion completed: new Terminal 2 inaugurated, capacity path to 80M passengers
tailwinds.relevance: Roughly 850M euro expansion adds 170,000 sqm of terminal space and targets 80M annual passengers, meaning more turns per day on the ramp and growing baggage-tow volume that handlers must staff or automate.

tailwinds.headline: EBRD lends Celebi 18M euro to electrify GSE at 10 Turkish airports including Antalya
tailwinds.relevance: An incumbent AYT handler is actively refreshing and electrifying its tractor fleet, exactly the procurement moment when a retrofit autonomy layer is cheapest to attach.

tailwinds.headline: Fraport-TAV secures 2.5B euro long-term refinancing for Antalya Airport
tailwinds.relevance: 13.5-year facility locks in the concessionaire's long-horizon investment in AYT, supporting multi-year operational technology programs rather than short-cycle decisions.

operationsNotes: AYT is operated by the Fraport-TAV joint venture under a long-term concession and is Turkey's busiest leisure gateway. First expansion phase completed with the new international Terminal 2 inaugurated April 12, 2025 (about 850M euro invested, ~440,000 sqm of terminal space, 80M annual pax capacity target) per https://www.fraport.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/2025/q2/antalya-airport--most-popular-holiday-gateway-in-the-mediterrane.html. Ground handling is handler_led: Turkey's three licensed national handlers (Havas, Celebi, TGS) provide ramp services to the largely foreign leisure/charter carrier base, and handlers own and are electrifying their own GSE fleets (Celebi EBRD loan covers Antalya) per https://www.ebrd.com/home/news-and-events/news/2025/ebrd-supports-electrification-of-celebi-aviation-s-ground-handli.html and https://www.lca.logcluster.org/turkiye-223-antalya-international-airport. Labor pressure: Antalya ATC staged a work-to-rule strike over pay and conditions in June 2024 causing multi-week delays (https://crisis24.garda.com/alerts/2024/06/turkiye-air-traffic-controllers-staging-work-to-rule-strike-at-antalya-airport-through-june-30 and https://www.eurasiareview.com/03072024-the-hidden-reason-behind-flight-delays-at-antalya-airport-oped/), and Turkish aviation labor costs are escalating fast under inflation-indexed CBAs (Hava-Is 28th-term agreement set a 64% wage increase for H1 2024 and CPI+10% adjustments through 2025, https://havais.org.tr/uploaded/collective-bargaining-agreement-for-the-28th-term-1744630779.pdf). No ramp-handler strike at AYT itself was found.
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE vendor found on this ramp. Attractive profile: Europe-facing leisure mega-hub expanding toward 80M pax capacity (Terminal 2 expansion opened April 2025), three competing licensed handlers (Havas, Celebi, TGS) with handler-owned GSE fleets, and Celebi already funding GSE electrification at AYT via an EBRD loan, which signals fleet-modernization budget that a retrofit RaaS pitch can ride. Constraints: extreme seasonality, Turkish lira/procurement dynamics, and no evidence yet of autonomy pilots anywhere in Turkey; Swissport/dnata/Menzies do not appear on this ramp so entry runs through Turkish handlers or Fraport-TAV. Celebi group relationship could also open its other 31 Turkish stations.

name: Athens International Airport (Eleftherios Venizelos)
owner: Athens International Airport S.A. (AviAlliance 50.2%, Greek state via HCAP 25.6%, free float; listed on the Athens Exchange since the Feb 2024 IPO)

groundHandlers.name: Goldair Handling
groundHandlers.notes: First private handler in the liberalized Greek market (since 1999); network of 28 Greek airports, 160+ airline clients incl. Aegean, Lufthansa, Emirates, Turkish.

groundHandlers.name: Skyserv Handling Services
groundHandlers.notes: Headquartered at ATH; acquired by Swissport Aviareps Hellas S.A. in Dec 2015, so Swissport-linked ownership.

groundHandlers.name: Swissport Hellas
groundHandlers.notes: One of the three HCAA-licensed ground handling operators at ATH (approval announced Sept 2022).

aerovectNotes: Large, fast-growing European hub with no incumbent autonomous-GSE vendor: clean whitespace. Handler-led market with three licensed operators; watch that Swissport Hellas ties into Swissport’s global Aurrigo alignment. AIA’s Digital Gate open-innovation program is a documented technology entry point.
operationsNotes: Greek ground handling has been liberalized since 1999; HCAA licenses exactly three third-party operators at ATH (Goldair, Skyserv, Swissport Hellas) and AIA awards and supervises handling without self-handling. Flight disruptions in 2024-2026 were driven by ATC actions and nationwide general strikes (e.g. Apr 9 and Oct 1, 2025), not by ground-handler disputes. Expansion program (~1.28B euros) targets 40M passengers by 2032 with a 68% terminal expansion; airport reached Net Zero in 2025 with electrified operational vehicles.

tailwinds.headline: ATH closes 2025 at a record ~34M passengers and launches a 1.28B euro expansion targeting 40M annual passengers by 2032
tailwinds.relevance: A growing, capacity-constrained hub adds ramp tempo and new apron capacity (32-position facility underway): more turns per tractor and a build window to design autonomy in.

tailwinds.headline: AIA: 2025 was the fastest growth among European capital/major airports vs pre-pandemic levels (+33% vs 2019, per ACI-Europe), with 283,589 flight movements (+5.7%)
tailwinds.relevance: Fastest-growing major European airport claim verified via AIA citing ACI-Europe: the growth story behind the GSE fleet expanding here.

tailwinds.headline: ATH reaches Net Zero in 2025 via its Route 2025 program (16 MWp solar park, 70M euro investment), 25 years ahead of the industry 2050 target
tailwinds.relevance: A sustainability-forward operator already electrifying airside vehicles is a natural adopter of electric retrofit autonomous tractors.

tailwinds.headline: AIA lists on the Athens Exchange: 785M euros raised at a 2.46B euro market cap, the largest Greek IPO in over two decades; AviAlliance takes majority control
tailwinds.relevance: Publicly listed, majority-private ownership (AviAlliance/PSP) means commercial capital discipline and appetite for efficiency investment.

sources: https://hcaa.gov.gr/en/newsroom/announcements/adeiodotisi-foreon-epigeias-exypiretisis (HCAA licensing of Goldair, Swissport Hellas, Skyserv at ATH)

name: Lisbon Humberto Delgado Airport

groundHandlers.name: SPdH/Menzies (Groundforce Portugal)
groundHandlers.role: Passenger, ramp, baggage and cargo handling at LIS
groundHandlers.notes: Menzies Aviation completed acquisition of 50.1% on 2024-06-04; TAP retains 49.9%. ~65% market share in Portugal, 4,000+ staff across 5 airports incl. Lisbon. Licence extended only to 2026-05-19 pending contested tender. Source: https://menziesaviation.com/news/menzies-aviation-completes-groundforce-portugal-deal/

groundHandlers.name: Portway Handling de Portugal
groundHandlers.role: Ramp, passenger, cargo handling at LIS
groundHandlers.notes: Owned 100% by ANA Aeroportos (Vinci Airports since 2013). Operates Lisbon, Porto, Faro, Funchal, Beja; 80+ airline clients; cargo handling leader. EUR 92.1M revenue in 2024. Source: https://www.portway.pt/en/company/portway/

groundHandlers.name: Clece/South consortium
groundHandlers.notes: ANAC preliminary award of new 7-year ground handling licences (Jan 2026) scored Clece/South above Menzies; Menzies filed injunction 2026-03-17, outcome unresolved as of mid-2026. Source: https://www.aman-alliance.org/Home/ContentDetail/101329

tailwinds.headline: ANAC ground handling tender upheaval: 7-year licences preliminarily awarded to Clece/South; Menzies files injunction; current licences extended to 19 May 2026
tailwinds.relevance: Contract change moment: an incoming handler needs differentiation and fleet decisions, and incumbents face cost/efficiency pressure, both openings for retrofit autonomy pitches

tailwinds.headline: Humberto Delgado Airport expansion works (Mota-Engil/Vinci, EUR 233M+) completing Terminal 2 phase Oct 2025, targeting 45 movements/hour by 2028
tailwinds.relevance: Capacity squeeze until the new Alcochete airport (2034 target) means ramp throughput gains via automation are directly valuable

tailwinds.headline: GSE Expo Europe returns to FIL Lisbon Sept 15-17, 2026, with Level 4 autonomous baggage tractors (Charlatte Autonom) exhibiting
tailwinds.relevance: Autonomous GSE showcased on Lisbon's doorstep normalizes the category with local handlers and ANA/Vinci; low-cost venue for AeroVect presence

operationsNotes: LIS handling is capped-licence, handler-led (Portugal licenses a limited set of ground handlers; TAP outsources to SPdH/Menzies, in which it keeps 49.9%). Repeated SPdH/Menzies strikes ran from Sept 2025 through the New Year period, with government-ordered minimum services; the Dec 31/Jan 1 walkout was called off after a shareholder agreement (sources: https://www.theportugalnews.com/news/2025-12-15/portugal-airport-staff-strike-announced/932023 ; https://www.portugalresident.com/airport-strike-called-off-after-agreement-with-former-groundforce-shareholders/). Press warns of possible summer 2026 handling disruption if the licence dispute is unresolved (https://theportugalpost.com/posts/summer-travel-alert-portugals-airport-ground-handling-crisis-may-disrupt-your-flight). Airport is congested; expansion works target 38 to 45 movements/hour by 2028.
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE vendor found deployed or trialing on the LIS ramp; targeted searches for TractEasy/EasyMile, Aurrigo, UISEE, AeroVect in Portugal returned nothing. Strong automation pull exists (repeated handler strikes, tender-driven cost pressure, capacity constraints) but the handler landscape is in flux: ANAC's 7-year licence award to Clece/South is under Menzies litigation with current licences expiring 2026-05-19, so the buying counterparty is unstable near term. Portway (Vinci-owned) is the stable second handler and a possible entry point. GSE Expo Europe runs in Lisbon Sept 15-17, 2026, a cheap way to be present in-market.

competitors.status: Not on LIS ramp; exhibiting/demoing Level 4 autonomous baggage tractor at GSE Expo Europe in Lisbon (demo Sept 2024, exhibiting again Sept 15-17, 2026)

name: Dublin Airport

groundHandlers.name: Aer Lingus (self-handling)
groundHandlers.notes: Provides its own passenger and baggage handling at DUB per daa ground handlers page (https://www.dublinairport.com/b2b/airport-suppliers/ground-handlers).

groundHandlers.name: Ryanair (self-handling)
groundHandlers.notes: Provides its own passenger and baggage handling at DUB per daa ground handlers page (https://www.dublinairport.com/b2b/airport-suppliers/ground-handlers).

groundHandlers.name: Sky Handling Partner
groundHandlers.notes: Groupe CRIT subsidiary; claims over 50% of the third-party handling market at Dublin; 20+ airline customers; ramp, baggage, pushback, de-icing (https://skyhandlingpartner.ie/).

groundHandlers.name: Menzies Aviation
groundHandlers.notes: Dublin station launched handling IAG carriers (British Airways, Vueling, Iberia Express) plus Icelandair; ~500 turnarounds in first two weeks (https://www.aviationpros.com/ground-handling/ground-handlers-service-providers/press-release/12414491/menzies-aviation-menzies-announces-successful-dublin-station-launch).

groundHandlers.name: Swissport
groundHandlers.notes: Lists Dublin (DUB) as an active station on its network site (https://www.swissport.com/en/network/europe/ireland/dub); page blocked direct fetch (403) so service scope unverified.

groundHandlers.name: WFS (Worldwide Flight Services)
groundHandlers.notes: WFS lists a Dublin, Ireland location (https://www.wfs.aero/locations/dublin-ireland/); scope not verified in detail.

tailwinds.headline: Irish government moves to end Dublin Airport 32M passenger cap via Dublin Airport (Passenger Capacity) Bill 2026
tailwinds.relevance: Cap removal unlocks traffic growth well beyond 36.4M pax, increasing turnaround volume and ramp productivity pressure that favors autonomous tractor economics.

tailwinds.headline: daa secures EUR 2.35M first instalment of EUR 4.7M EU CEF AFIF funding for Dublin Airport electrification, including charging infrastructure for ground service equipment
tailwinds.relevance: Airside EV charging build-out lowers the infrastructure barrier for electric autonomous tow tractors and signals daa appetite for GSE modernization.

tailwinds.headline: daa projects ~40M passengers by 2030 and is progressing an infrastructure application for new piers and aircraft stands after the cap lift
tailwinds.relevance: New stands and piers expand the airside footprint and tow distances, strengthening the case for automated baggage/cargo tugs during the growth phase.

operationsNotes: DUB handled 36.4M passengers in 2025, above its 32M planning cap; enforcement stayed by the High Court while government legislates removal (https://www.rte.ie/news/2026/0215/1558529-dublin-airport-cap/). Two-terminal operation: Ryanair at T1, Aer Lingus at T2, both self-handling; third-party market led by Sky Handling Partner with Menzies and Swissport also present. Labor signals: SIPTU members at Sky Handling Partner (baggage/check-in for Qatar, Etihad, Delta, Air France, KLM) balloted on industrial action in May 2024 over union recognition (https://www.rte.ie/news/business/2024/0523/1450836-siptu-airport/, https://www.thejournal.ie/dublin-airport-baggage-strike-6387397-May2024/); separately, Irish airspace over Dublin closed repeatedly in early 2026 due to ATC staffing shortages, an aviation-wide staffing strain signal though not ramp-specific (https://www.thejournal.ie/airspace-closed-dublin-airport-staffing-6957484-Feb2026/).
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE trials or deployments found at DUB after multiple targeted searches (TractEasy, Aurrigo, EasyMile, UISEE, generic autonomous baggage tractor). A Sept 2025 Irish tech piece confirms autonomy remains university research (Trinity College Dublin LIDAR baggage-train demos), not airport operations. Ramp is open: mixed self-handling (Aer Lingus, Ryanair) plus third-party handlers gives multiple entry paths. Note Swissport and Menzies are on this ramp; Aurrigo partners with Swissport elsewhere (ZRH), so that channel is the main competitive watch item. Strong catalysts: 36.4M pax in 2025, cap removal in progress, funded GSE charging infrastructure.

name: Melbourne Airport

groundHandlers.name: Swissport
groundHandlers.role: Ramp, baggage, pushback, cargo loading for Qantas
groundHandlers.notes: Handled Qantas domestic at MEL since 2021; expanded April 2024 to entire Qantas narrow-body and wide-body fleet incl. international, 750+ flights/week. Melbourne hub fleet includes 18 electric baggage tugs bought largely for the Qantas growth (payloadasia.com, swissport.com).

groundHandlers.name: dnata
groundHandlers.notes: Operates ground handling at Melbourne; MEL workers took part in Dec 2024/early 2025 strike action over pay before a Feb 2025 enterprise agreement (australianaviation.com.au, dnata.com).

groundHandlers.name: Menzies Aviation
groundHandlers.role: Passenger, ramp and cargo services for multiple international carriers
groundHandlers.notes: Renewed MEL contracts in Oct 2024 round: United (cargo to 2029), Malaysia Airlines (pax/ramp/cargo to 2029), China Airlines, Thai Airways (pax/ramp), Air India cargo warehouse (aviationpros.com).

tailwinds.headline: Melbourne Airport third runway: federal approval Sept 2024, early construction works began Aug 2025, A$3B project targeting 2031 opening
tailwinds.relevance: Major capacity expansion means more aircraft movements and more baggage/cargo tows per day, growing the addressable tow volume for autonomous tractors.

tailwinds.headline: Swissport wins Qantas' entire MEL fleet (750+ flights/week) and invests A$5M+ in electric GSE, incl. 18 electric baggage tugs at its Melbourne hub
tailwinds.relevance: Consolidated handler-led contract plus a young electric tug fleet at MEL is a direct retrofit target for AeroVect's RaaS model; Swissport is also an Aurrigo partner elsewhere, so autonomy is on its roadmap.

tailwinds.headline: dnata ground handlers lock in new enterprise agreement with pay rises up to 12% over two years after strike action at Sydney, Melbourne, Adelaide and Brisbane
tailwinds.relevance: Double-digit ramp wage escalation and demonstrated strike risk at MEL sharpen the labor-cost and resilience ROI case for autonomous tugs.

operationsNotes: Qantas fully outsourced MEL ground handling (Swissport since 2020-21, whole fleet from 2024) after its 2020 outsourcing of ~2,000 ground jobs, which the TWU continues to contest on standards grounds. Over 1,000 dnata ramp/baggage/cargo workers at Sydney, Melbourne, Adelaide and Brisbane voted 98% for protected action; a 24-hour strike was planned Dec 2024 and a 4-hour walkout occurred before dnata settled in Feb 2025 with pay rises up to 12% over two years plus rostering improvements. TWU-dnata friction continued into Aug 2025 (Perth pay dispute).
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE vendor (TractEasy, Aurrigo, UISEE or other) found trialing or deployed at MEL; Aurrigo's only Australian activity is a non-airport cargo pod test at Tonsley Innovation District, SA. Ramp is handler_led with three global handlers (Swissport, dnata, Menzies) that AeroVect already knows from other markets. Swissport is actively electrifying its MEL tug fleet (18 electric baggage tugs), a retrofit-friendly signal. Double-digit ground-handler wage settlements and recent strike action strengthen the autonomy ROI case, and the A$3B third runway (open ~2031) adds long-run movement growth.

name: Detroit Metropolitan Wayne County Airport

groundHandlers.name: Delta Air Lines (in-house)
groundHandlers.notes: Delta directly hires DTW ramp agents for below-wing work at its hub. Source: https://www.ziprecruiter.com/c/Delta-Air-Lines,-Inc./Job/Ramp-Agent-(Customer-Service-Agent)-DTW/-in-Detroit,MI?jid=b8550927e70a0c4e

groundHandlers.name: Unifi
groundHandlers.notes: Active DTW postings for Delta full-handling ramp agents; Delta holds ~49% of Unifi. Unifi also operates the DTW autonomous wheelchair program. Source: https://careers.unifiservice.com/careers/JobDetail/3596-DTW-Delta-Ramp-Service-RD2AGT-Ramp-Agent-Driving-FT-Agent-AI/12174

groundHandlers.name: Menzies Aviation
groundHandlers.notes: Current Menzies network page lists DTW as an active ground services station. Source: https://menziesaviation.com/our-network/detroit-metropolitan-wayne-county-dtw/

groundHandlers.name: Worldwide Flight Services (WFS)
groundHandlers.notes: WFS lists a Detroit location in its network. Source: https://www.wfs.aero/locations/detroit-us/

groundHandlers.name: Swissport
groundHandlers.notes: IAM Local 698 members at Swissport DTW struck in April 2022; current presence not verified. Source: https://www.goiam.org/news/imail/detroit-local-698-members-at-swissport-on-strike-for-respect-and-dignity/

groundHandlers.name: G2 Secure Staff
groundHandlers.notes: Hiring DTW ramp agents at $15.50/hr via job fairs. Source: https://www.semcamiworks.org/em-event/detroit-metro-airport-g2-secure-staff-job-fair/

tailwinds.headline: DTW expands Unifi-operated autonomous wheelchairs to Evans Terminal
tailwinds.relevance: Wayne County Airport Authority and Unifi are already operating autonomous vehicles at DTW, proving stakeholder appetite and an approval pathway for autonomy on site.

tailwinds.headline: DTW and Unifi launch WHILL autonomous wheelchair pilot with Michigan Economic Development Corp grant
tailwinds.relevance: Unifi leadership framed automation as relief for staff strain at peak times, and Michigan state mobility funding is available for autonomy pilots at DTW.

tailwinds.headline: Delta 2025 ESG report: 42% of core GSE fleet electrified, targeting 100% electric core GSE at hubs by 2035
tailwinds.relevance: GSE fleet turnover to electric at Delta hubs like DTW creates a natural retrofit and replacement window for autonomous tractor deployment.

operationsNotes: Mixed handling model: Delta self-handles mainline below-wing with directly hired DTW ramp agents while Unifi also performs Delta full handling (likely regional/overflow); Menzies, WFS (cargo), G2, and possibly Swissport serve other carriers. Two terminals (McNamara for Delta/SkyTeam, Evans Terminal). Winter deicing operation is significant. TSA staffing strain during the 2026 shutdown did not disrupt DTW operations per local reporting.
aerovectNotes: Delta hub with large baggage operation and no autonomous-GSE competitor found on the ramp. Unifi, a key US handler, is present at scale and already runs an autonomy program (WHILL wheelchairs) with the airport authority and Michigan state funding, so both the handler and WCAA have shown willingness to approve autonomous vehicles. Delta eGSE electrification creates a retrofit entry point. Entry paths: Delta in-house ramp (carrier-led deal) or Unifi (handler-led deal).

competitors.vendor: WHILL (autonomous wheelchairs, adjacent only, not ramp GSE)
competitors.status: deployed terminal-side: 10-chair pilot with Unifi in McNamara Terminal April 2025, expanded to Evans Terminal April 2026; no autonomous baggage tractor vendor found on the DTW ramp

name: Cancún International Airport

groundHandlers.name: Swissport
groundHandlers.role: Ramp handling, passenger services, baggage, fueling, and cargo warehouse
groundHandlers.notes: Serving airlines at CUN since 1999 per Swissport's official CUN station page (swissport.com/en/network/north-america/mexico/cun).

groundHandlers.name: Menzies Aviation
groundHandlers.role: Ramp, passenger, and cabin cleaning services for multiple airlines
groundHandlers.notes: CUN is a listed Menzies network station. Contracts include Aeroflot (passenger, ramp, cabin cleaning), Frontier, Flair, and TAG Airlines per Menzies press releases and trade press.

tailwinds.headline: ASUR expands Cancun Terminal 4 with US$467M investment, doubling terminal capacity and extending aprons
tailwinds.relevance: Apron extensions and more aircraft positions mean more tow/haul distance and more GSE demand on a ramp already at record traffic, strengthening the case for autonomous retrofit tractors.

tailwinds.headline: ASUR starts Terminal 1 rebuild at Cancun, operational by second half of 2026; Terminal 4 phase 2 completing end of 2025
tailwinds.relevance: Concurrent terminal projects with 6-8% annual passenger growth expectations increase ramp complexity and staffing needs, a classic window for introducing autonomous GSE as operations rescale.

tailwinds.headline: Mexico air traffic controllers file strike notice July 1 amid staffing deficit ahead of 2026 World Cup demand
tailwinds.relevance: National aviation labor unrest and World Cup traffic surge raise operational continuity concerns for Mexican airports including Cancun, making labor-resilient automation more attractive to handlers.

operationsNotes: CUN is operated by ASUR and is Mexico's second busiest airport (32.7M passengers in 2023). Ramp work is handled by competing third-party handlers (Swissport, Menzies, plus smaller local firms listed in directories such as SEAT and Aviation Support, which were not independently verified). No evidence found of carrier self-handling dominance, so handler_led is the best-supported model. Terminal 4 apron positions are being expanded from 26 as part of the current capex cycle.
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE vendor (TractEasy/EasyMile, Aurrigo, UISEE, or others) found trialing or deployed at CUN or anywhere in Mexico in searches run 2026-07. Ramp is open. Two global handlers with existing autonomy exposure elsewhere (Swissport works with Aurrigo at ZRH) operate at CUN, offering a handler-led entry path. Heavy 2024-2028 expansion capex, apron growth, and 2026 World Cup traffic pressure make it attractive, though Mexican airside regulation for autonomous vehicles is unverified.

name: LaGuardia Airport

groundHandlers.name: Swissport
groundHandlers.role: Third-party ramp handler and cabin cleaning; serves Air Canada and Spirit at LGA
groundHandlers.notes: Swissport lists LGA as a ramp handling station; has served LGA since 1979 per its network page (swissport.com LGA page).

groundHandlers.name: Unifi Aviation
groundHandlers.role: Contracted ramp and cabin services, primarily Delta work
groundHandlers.notes: Active Unifi ramp and cabin agent job postings at LaGuardia (ZipRecruiter, avg ramp pay ~$17.32/hr as of June 2026). Delta holds ~49% minority stake in Unifi, a warm channel for AeroVect.

groundHandlers.name: Delta Air Lines (self-handling)
groundHandlers.role: Carrier-led ramp at Terminal C hub
groundHandlers.notes: Delta ramp agents handle ~8,000 checked bags daily at LGA Terminal C per an April 2025 gvwire feature; Delta operates its own $4B consolidated Terminal C.

tailwinds.headline: Port Authority and LaGuardia Gateway Partners to install 164 new eGSE charging ports at Terminal B, completion 2027
tailwinds.relevance: More than doubles electric GSE charging capacity across Terminal B concourses and baggage areas under the Port Authority net-zero 2050 program; handlers replacing diesel tractors with new electric units is a natural moment to spec retrofit autonomy.

tailwinds.headline: Delta completes $4B LaGuardia Terminal C rebuild two years ahead of schedule (final gates opened Dec 2024)
tailwinds.relevance: 37-gate consolidated hub with modern ramp and baggage infrastructure gives a clean, standardized airside environment for autonomous baggage tractor routes; Delta's Unifi stake links carrier and handler decision-making.

operationsNotes: Mixed GSE model: Delta self-handles its Terminal C hub with its own ramp agents (gvwire, Apr 2025), Unifi provides contracted ramp/cabin labor at LGA (ZipRecruiter postings, 2026), and Swissport handles third-party carriers including Air Canada and Spirit (swissport.com LGA station page; Aviation Pros, 2023). Terminal C has a rebuilt baggage system with 3+ miles of conveyor across 37 gates.
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE vendor found on the LGA ramp (searches for AeroVect, TractEasy, Aurrigo, UISEE and generic airside autonomy trials at LGA returned nothing). Attractive: brand-new terminals (Terminal B 2022, Delta Terminal C fully complete end of 2024), 164-port eGSE charging buildout at Terminal B by 2027 creating a fleet-refresh window, Unifi presence (Delta minority owner) as a handler-led entry path, and high NYC ramp labor cost and union pressure strengthening the retrofit ROI case. Watch factor: LGA is a dense, space-constrained short-haul ramp with no known Port Authority autonomy program.

name: Cairo International Airport

groundHandlers.name: EgyptAir Ground Services
groundHandlers.role: Dominant ramp and passenger handler at CAI; subsidiary of state-owned EgyptAir Holding, handles home carrier plus third-party airlines
groundHandlers.notes: Hosted the 38th IATA Ground Handling Conference in Cairo, May 2026. Source: https://flock.aero/companies/1303-egyptair-ground-services and https://www.iata.org/en/pressroom/2026-releases/2026-04-15-01/

groundHandlers.name: Egyptian Aviation Services (EAS)
groundHandlers.role: Second ground handler; ramp, passenger, cargo and lounge services at major Egyptian airports incl. Cairo
groundHandlers.notes: Established 1989 by the Egyptian government. Source: https://eas-eg.com/

groundHandlers.name: Aero Services Egypt (ASE)
groundHandlers.role: ISAGO-certified handler with main station at Cairo International
groundHandlers.notes: Source: https://www.asegrp.aero/locations/Cairo-International-Airport

groundHandlers.name: ZAS Aviation Services
groundHandlers.role: Executive/general aviation and supervisory handling at CAI
groundHandlers.notes: Source: https://www.zasair.com/

tailwinds.headline: Egypt unveils $3.5B aviation overhaul: Cairo Terminal 4 to lift capacity to ~30M pax plus new 1M-ton cargo city
tailwinds.relevance: Major capacity growth means more turnarounds and more tug movements on the CAI ramp; modernization budget creates an opening for new-generation GSE

tailwinds.headline: Cairo hosted the 38th IATA Ground Handling Conference, themed 'Adapting Ground Operations in an Era of AI', hosted by EgyptAir
tailwinds.relevance: Puts ground-ops automation and AI on the agenda of the exact CAI stakeholders (EgyptAir Ground Services, Ministry of Civil Aviation) AeroVect would sell to

tailwinds.headline: EgyptAir Ground Services to convert ground handling equipment to electric power by 2028
tailwinds.relevance: Fleet-wide GSE replacement cycle is the natural moment to add retrofit autonomy; signals the dominant handler is actively investing in its tractor fleet

operationsNotes: CAI is Egypt's largest hub. Handling is handler_led: EgyptAir Ground Services (state airline subsidiary acting as the dominant third-party handler), EAS, ASE and ZAS provide ramp services and own the GSE; foreign carriers buy handling rather than running their own ramps. Separate IFC-advised PPP program covers 11 other Egyptian airports (Hurghada first), not CAI (https://www.ifc.org/en/pressroom/2025/egypt-partners-with-ifc-to-introduce-public-private-partnerships-at-11-airports).
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE vendor found on the CAI ramp, and the modernization push (electric GSE by 2028, Terminal 4, IGHC 2026 AI theme) is real. But handling is dominated by state-owned EgyptAir Ground Services and EAS, procurement is government-led, and Egypt's low ramp labor costs weaken the RaaS labor-substitution ROI. Interesting to track via the electric-GSE conversion program; not an obvious near-term prospect.

name: Vienna International Airport

groundHandlers.name: Vienna Airport Handling Services (Flughafen Wien AG division)
groundHandlers.role: Dominant full-service handler: ramp, passenger, cargo, load control, de-icing (10 Safeaero + 5 Vestergaard Elephant BETA rigs)
groundHandlers.notes: In-house handling division of the airport operator itself. Source: https://www.viennaairport.com/en/business__partner/aviation__handling/ground_handling

groundHandlers.name: Celebi Ground Services Austria GmbH
groundHandlers.role: Second licensed handler: full passenger and ramp handling (loading/unloading, pushback/towing, crew transport, cleaning); also operates the boarding bridges at VIE
groundHandlers.notes: Operating at VIE since 2011; only non-airport company offering complete passenger plus ramp service. Sources: http://www.celebihandling.at/en/corporate and https://www.celebiaviation.com/about

groundHandlers.name: Swissport
groundHandlers.role: Cargo warehouse operations only (two air cargo warehouses), not ramp handling
groundHandlers.notes: At VIE since 2008. Source: https://www.swissport.com/en/network/europe/austria/vie

tailwinds.headline: Vienna Airport caps record-breaking 2025 with 32-millionth passenger (full-year 32.6M)
tailwinds.relevance: Record ramp volumes stress baggage/cargo tug capacity and strengthen the throughput case for autonomous tractor retrofits.

tailwinds.headline: Record EUR 330M infrastructure investment for 2026 incl. Terminal 3 southern expansion (70,000 m2, opening Q2 2027) alongside strict cost-cutting on personnel expenses
tailwinds.relevance: Expansion adds gates and tow distances while management explicitly targets personnel cost reduction, a direct opening for RaaS autonomy on existing tractors. Expansion detail: https://viennaairport.com/en/business__partner/infrastructure/suederweiterung

tailwinds.headline: Austrian airport/ground-handling collective agreement adds +3.3% wages from 1 May 2025, after +7.0% in May 2024, for 6,500 workers
tailwinds.relevance: Compounding double-digit ramp labor cost inflation over two years improves the ROI math for autonomous GSE.

operationsNotes: GSE is handler_led: Vienna Airport Handling Services is a division of the airport operator Flughafen Wien AG and provides most ramp/baggage handling (https://www.viennaairport.com/en/business__partner/aviation__handling/ground_handling); Celebi Ground Services Austria is the second full ramp handler since 2011 and also runs the boarding bridges (http://www.celebihandling.at/en/corporate). Labor: no VIE ground-staff strikes found (2024 Austrian Airlines strikes were airline crew, not ramp), but sector-wide Austrian airport/ground-handling collective agreements delivered +7.0% wages effective 1 May 2024 for 6,500 workers (https://www.gpa.at/kollektivvertrag/transport-und-verkehr/oeffentliche-flughaefen/kollektivvertrag-2024-fuer-oeffentliche-flughaefen) and +3.3% effective 1 May 2025 (https://www.vida.at/de/artikel/luft-und-schiffsverkehr/2025/flughafen-kv-2025); airport management is running a strict cost-cutting program on personnel expenses for 2026 (https://aviation.direct/en/Vienna-Airport:-record-year-2025-and-outlook-for-a-challenging-business-year-2026).
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE vendor found on the VIE ramp (searches for EasyMile/TractEasy, Aurrigo, UISEE and generic autonomous baggage tractor activity at VIE returned nothing). Ramp is handler_led with the airport operator's own division as dominant handler, so one buyer controls most of the tractor fleet. Entry logic: management announced a strict cost-cutting program on operating and personnel expenses for 2026 while ramp wages compounded +7.0% (May 2024) and +3.3% (May 2025) under the national airport KV; an opex-based retrofit RaaS pitch fits that squeeze. Celebi (a known major handler globally) is a second entry path. Automation appetite is demonstrated terminal-side (autonomous wheelchair PoC citing staff shortage, per https://cities-today.com/vienna-airport-set-to-trial-autonomous-wheelchairs/) and the airport already runs ~400 electric GSE units. Caveat: 2026 traffic is guided down to ~30M pax on Ryanair/Wizz capacity cuts, which may slow discretionary projects.

name: Palma de Mallorca Airport

groundHandlers.name: Groundforce (Globalia)
groundHandlers.role: Third-party ramp handler, one of three 7-year Aena licenses 2024-2031
groundHandlers.notes: Retained PMI in the Sept 2023 Aena tender; incumbent Iberia lost Mallorca. Sources: preferente.com and eleconomista.es tender coverage.

groundHandlers.name: Menzies Aviation
groundHandlers.role: Third-party ramp and passenger handler, Aena license 2024-2031
groundHandlers.notes: Won PMI in the ~EUR 5B Aena tender; committed to brand-new electric GSE with telematics. Handles easyJet, BA, Turkish, Norwegian, Wizz per local press. Source: menziesaviation.com.

groundHandlers.name: Swissport
groundHandlers.notes: New entrant at PMI in 2024 with ~300 staff and a fleet reported ~64.9% electric. Source: swissport.com PMI launch release (found via search; page blocked direct fetch).

groundHandlers.name: Azul Handling (Ryanair Group)
groundHandlers.notes: Ryanair's in-house Spanish handler operates at PMI; center of the Aug-Dec 2025 strike wave. Source: palmaairport.info.

tailwinds.headline: Palma Airport enters final stretch of EUR 560M redevelopment, completion targeted summer 2026
tailwinds.relevance: Major modernization program with carbon-neutrality goals and 65 concurrent projects signals appetite for new airside technology and updated infrastructure that autonomous tractors can slot into.

tailwinds.headline: PMI closes 2025 with record 33.8M passengers and 246,486 movements
tailwinds.relevance: Record, heavily seasonal volumes on a strike-prone ramp increase baggage-flow pressure and the ROI of autonomy that absorbs summer peaks without added headcount.

tailwinds.headline: Swissport commits ~EUR 1.5B over five years to electric GSE and fleet electrification
tailwinds.relevance: Swissport holds one of PMI's three ramp licenses; a global fleet-renewal cycle creates a natural window to add retrofit autonomy on new electric tractors.

operationsNotes: Ramp is handler-led for third-party work (Groundforce, Menzies, Swissport own their GSE under Aena licenses) with carrier self-handling by Ryanair via Azul Handling, so model is mixed. Azul Handling ran rolling strikes at PMI and 11 other Spanish airports from 2025-08-15 through 2025-12-31 (Wed/Fri/Sat/Sun stoppages) over pay parity, guaranteed hours, and job insecurity; Menzies PMI staff (UGT) threatened Aug 2025 strikes, suspended after a draft deal on shifts, hours, and progression. Highly seasonal traffic peaks (Germany and UK markets ~15.8M pax combined) strain summer ramp staffing.
aerovectNotes: Spain's third-busiest airport (33.8M pax 2025, record) with no autonomous-GSE vendor found on the ramp. Three fresh 7-year handler licenses (2024-2031) came with electric-GSE investment commitments, so fleets are new and telematics-equipped, a good retrofit target. High seasonality plus sustained handling strikes (Azul Aug-Dec 2025, Menzies near-strike Aug 2025) strengthen the automation case. Constraint to plan for: handling access is governed by Aena's centralized license regime, so entry likely runs through Menzies, Swissport, or Groundforce corporate rather than the airport.

name: Noi Bai International Airport

groundHandlers.name: VIAGS (Vietnam Airport Ground Services, Noi Bai branch)
groundHandlers.notes: Vietnam Airlines subsidiary formed 2016 from merger incl. Noi Bai Intl Airport Ground Services; 1,700+ staff and 600+ GSE units at Noi Bai per viags.vn; ISAGO certified (https://www.viags.vn/en-US/about-viags, https://centreforaviation.com/data/profiles/suppliers/vietnam-airport-ground-services)

groundHandlers.name: HGS (Hanoi Ground Services JSC)
groundHandlers.role: Ramp handling, passenger handling, load control
groundHandlers.notes: IATA GHP member at HAN (https://centreforaviation.com/data/profiles/suppliers/hanoi-ground-services-company-hgs, https://www.iata.org/en/publications/directories/ghp-directory/hanoi-ground-services-joint-stock-company/113/)

groundHandlers.name: NCTS (Noi Bai Cargo Terminal Services JSC)
groundHandlers.notes: Vietnam Airlines subsidiary founded 2005; cargo terminal operator at HAN (https://centreforaviation.com/data/profiles/suppliers/noi-bai-cargo-terminal-services, https://www.iata.org/en/publications/directories/ghp-directory/noi-bai-cargo-terminal-services-joint-stock-company-ncts/153/)

groundHandlers.name: Airport NEO Ground Services (Vietjet subsidiary)
groundHandlers.role: Full ground support incl. ramp operations and load control
groundHandlers.notes: Handled Air India's inaugural scheduled Hanoi flight May 2026; states plans to invest in infrastructure, equipment and talent (https://en.vietnamplus.vn/vietjet-ground-handling-arm-serves-global-airline-as-brand-soars-worldwide-post342328.vnp)

tailwinds.headline: Expanded Terminal T2 at Noi Bai inaugurated, international capacity up from 10M to 15M pax/yr
tailwinds.relevance: More gates (17 to 30), jet bridges (14 to 27) and traffic mean more baggage/cargo tows per day on a ramp already short of skilled ground-ops labor, strengthening the case for autonomous tractor retrofits

tailwinds.headline: Noi Bai's expanded international terminal launches Vietnam's first fully integrated digital passenger flow (kiosk check-in, self bag drop, biometric security, automated immigration)
tailwinds.relevance: Shows ACV/Noi Bai leadership is actively investing in operational automation, a cultural and budgetary precursor to airside/GSE automation

tailwinds.headline: Vietjet's Airport NEO Ground Services begins handling third-party international carriers at Noi Bai (Air India inaugural flight) and plans investment in equipment and infrastructure
tailwinds.relevance: A growing handler competing for third-party contracts with stated GSE investment plans is a natural retrofit-autonomy prospect seeking cost and reliability differentiation

operationsNotes: Post-expansion HAN capacity is ~30M pax/yr (T1 domestic 15M + T2 international 15M, flexible to 18M); T2 expansion cost ~USD 190-200M and added 13 jet bridges and 13 boarding gates (https://en.vietnamplus.vn/expanded-terminal-t2-at-noi-bai-international-airport-makes-debut-post334592.vnp). GSE is operated by handling companies, but the dominant handlers are airline subsidiaries, so the model is handler_led with strong carrier-group influence. Vietnam's aviation sector reports shortages in ground operations management and other skilled roles against a ~44,000-person workforce (https://en.vietnamplus.vn/aviation-industry-faces-human-resource-shortage-post266393.vnp). No strike activity at HAN found in sources reviewed.
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE vendor activity found at HAN, so the ramp is open. Attractive volume story: ~30M pax combined capacity after the Dec 2025 T2 expansion and country-level ground-ops labor shortage. Constraint: all major handlers are airline-owned (VIAGS and NCTS under Vietnam Airlines, Airport NEO under Vietjet), so procurement runs through state-linked or carrier-group channels rather than independent handlers. Airport NEO's stated equipment-investment plans and the airport's terminal-automation push are the nearest openings.

name: Philadelphia International Airport

groundHandlers.name: American Airlines
groundHandlers.notes: PHL is a major AA hub with ~380 daily departures (183 mainline) and ~10,000 ops employees under VP Ops Cesar Marchese; rebanked to 7 banks April 2026. Source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/tedreed/2026/06/12/american-airlines-reboots-hub-in-philly-origin-for-us-250th-birthday/

groundHandlers.name: PrimeFlight Aviation Services
groundHandlers.notes: Provides baggage, wheelchair, skycap and counter services in every PHL terminal; workers unionized with SEIU 32BJ. PrimeFlight acquired AeroVect partner GAT in Feb 2026. Sources: https://www.aviationpros.com/ground-handling/ground-handlers-service-providers/news/10937964/primeflight-faces-osha-complaint and https://www.aircargonews.net/acquisitions/2026/02/primeflight-acquires-gat-to-expand-ground-handling-services/

groundHandlers.name: Swissport USA
groundHandlers.notes: Swissport USA lists a Philadelphia station. Source: https://marketplace.aviationweek.com/company/swissport-usa-inc-philadelphia/

tailwinds.headline: American Airlines reboots PHL hub: 7 banks, schedule up ~10%, new intl routes ahead of US 250th and World Cup
tailwinds.relevance: More departures and tighter banked peaks raise ramp throughput and turn-time pressure; AA cites baggage delivery as a key metric, the exact workflow autonomous tractors address.

tailwinds.headline: PHL finishing $500M in terminal upgrades by May 2026 for FIFA World Cup and 250th anniversary crowds
tailwinds.relevance: Major capital spend and a summer 2026 traffic surge (6 World Cup matches, MLB All-Star Game) increase pressure on ground operations capacity.

tailwinds.headline: PHL and Southwest electrify GSE: 24 charging positions at Terminal E, electric fleet reaches 26%
tailwinds.relevance: Electric tug fleet and charging infrastructure are directly adjacent to autonomous EV tractor deployments and show airport willingness to modernize GSE (note: slightly older than 24 months).

operationsNotes: AA hub rebanked to 7 banks in April 2026 with schedule up ~10% YoY (https://www.forbes.com/sites/tedreed/2026/06/12/american-airlines-reboots-hub-in-philly-origin-for-us-250th-birthday/). PHL has 12 eGSE chargers / 24 charging positions at Terminal E and 26% of the GSE fleet electric (https://www.phl.org/newsroom/electric-GSEs). Long-run cargo expansion program announced 2021: +136 acres, cargo buildings from 600K to 1.4M sq ft (https://www.phl.org/newsroom/cargo-expansion). Labor: DC33 city workers handling airport custodial/maintenance struck July 1, 2025 during holiday peak (https://www.fox29.com/news/phl-grapples-striking-workers-storm-delays-uptick-holiday-travel-tips-remain-patient); subcontracted baggage/wheelchair workers (incl. PrimeFlight) have a history of strikes and 32BJ organizing at PHL (https://www.aviationpros.com/ground-handling/ground-handlers-service-providers/news/12324097/philadelphia-airport-workers-elect-to-unionize).
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE vendor found on the PHL ramp. Attractive: large growing AA hub, eGSE charging infrastructure already installed, and PrimeFlight (which acquired AeroVect partner GAT in Feb 2026) operates in every terminal, a possible channel. Biggest fleet is AA self-handled, so a carrier-led sale is needed for the core hub operation.

name: Zürich Airport

groundHandlers.name: Swissport
groundHandlers.notes: Zurich is Swissport’s home market; Swissport is the world’s #1 handler

aerovectNotes: Swissport (world #1 handler) is running its first global autonomous ground-handling pilot here with Aurrigo (announced May 2025, simulation phase first, live Auto-DollyTug trial as the next phase). Contested, not settled: retrofit-as-a-service is the counter-position if the pilot underdelivers.

competitors.status: live trial (pilot)
competitors.source: Swissport-Aurrigo ZRH pilot announced May 2025, starting with the Auto-Sim digital simulation phase; Aurrigo (Mar 2026) lists Zurich among eight live trials, not commercial deployments. https://www.airport-suppliers.com/aurrigo-international-brings-auto-dolly-tug-and-auto-shuttle-to-pte-world-2026/

tailwinds.headline: Swissport and Aurrigo launch first global pilot of autonomous ground handling at ZRH
tailwinds.relevance: Signals handler appetite for autonomy; the account is winnable if Aurrigo underdelivers

name: Manchester Airport

groundHandlers.name: dnata
groundHandlers.notes: Operates a fleet of 28 electric baggage tugs at MAN; consolidated all GSE charging into a single airside hub with Fronius (26 chargers), Dec 2025.

groundHandlers.name: Swissport
groundHandlers.role: Third-party ramp, passenger, baggage and cargo handler
groundHandlers.notes: Maintains a MAN station (ramp, passenger, fueling, baggage); one of the UK's two largest third-party handlers per the DfT ground handling review.

groundHandlers.name: Menzies Aviation
groundHandlers.role: Third-party ramp, passenger, cleaning and de-icing handler
groundHandlers.notes: Handles Aer Lingus at MAN (contract expanded with electric widebody GSE), reappointed by SunExpress in Nov 2024, and won Loganair handling at MAN.

groundHandlers.name: Jet2.com
groundHandlers.notes: Runs its own in-house ramp and baggage teams at Manchester as part of a deliberate self-handling strategy across its UK bases.

tailwinds.headline: dnata and Fronius build consolidated airside EV charging hub at MAN for 28 electric baggage tugs
tailwinds.relevance: A major MAN handler has fully electrified its tug fleet and tripled charging capacity (9 to 26 chargers) in one airside hub; electric tugs with modern battery telemetry are the natural platform for retrofit autonomy.

tailwinds.headline: Manchester Airport's GBP 1.3bn Terminal 2 transformation nears completion; ~80% of passengers to use expanded T2
tailwinds.relevance: Hold-baggage capacity more than doubles to 5,000+ bags per hour and operations consolidate on the expanded T2 ramp, increasing tug movement density and the payoff from autonomous baggage tows.

tailwinds.headline: UK-first trial of an autonomous excavator on a car park build at Manchester Airport
tailwinds.relevance: Airport operator MAG is already hosting autonomous vehicle trials on its estate, signaling institutional openness to autonomy pilots that eases the approval path for airside autonomous GSE.

operationsNotes: Around 130 Aer Lingus UK ground staff at MAN Terminal 2 (check-in, boarding, baggage) balloted for strike action over pay in Oct 2025. dnata catering workers at eight UK airports including Manchester balloted for strikes in Nov 2024 over changes to terms and conditions. UK ground handling has a recent pattern of handler pay disputes (dnata and Menzies strikes at Heathrow, Nov 2024).
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE vendor found on the MAN ramp after targeted searches (Aurrigo, TractEasy/EasyMile, UISEE, AeroVect). Nearest UK competitor activity is Aurrigo's Ramp Ready airside logistics project at East Midlands Airport, not MAN. Attractive entry points: dnata already runs an all-electric tug fleet with new consolidated charging (natural base for retrofit autonomy), plus Swissport, Menzies and self-handling Jet2 as separate buyer paths on one large UK hub.

name: London Stansted Airport

groundHandlers.name: Blue Handling (OmniServ / ABM Aviation)
groundHandlers.role: Ramp, baggage, check-in, de-icing and cargo handling dedicated to Ryanair, STN's dominant carrier
groundHandlers.notes: Took over all Ryanair above- and below-wing handling from Swissport on 1 Feb 2019 after baggage-delay problems; operation built with nearly 1,000 staff (corporate.ryanair.com, groundhandlinginternational.com)

groundHandlers.name: Swissport
groundHandlers.role: Ramp, passenger, baggage, fueling services plus an air cargo warehouse
groundHandlers.notes: Serving airlines at STN since 2013; lost the Ryanair contract in 2019 but retains other carriers and cargo (swissport.com STN page, search result)

groundHandlers.name: Menzies Aviation
groundHandlers.notes: Operates dedicated cargo facility with AOG support, pharma cold storage, express freight (tclogistics.uk/airports/stansted)

groundHandlers.name: dnata
groundHandlers.notes: One of three cargo facility operators at STN, which handles ~75,000 tonnes of air cargo annually (tclogistics.uk/airports/stansted)

groundHandlers.name: ABM
groundHandlers.notes: Same parent group as OmniServ/Blue Handling; ABM PRM workforce is the subject of 2026 strike action (unitetheunion.org)

tailwinds.headline: £1.1 billion investment to expand Stansted Airport welcomed by ministers
tailwinds.relevance: Terminal expanded by a third, capacity rising from ~30M to 43M passengers and 5,000+ jobs; more turns and baggage volume increase demand for ramp automation, construction starting 2025 (see also newcivilengineer.com, 2025-05-23)

tailwinds.headline: UK aviation leaders launch Airside Automation Group
tailwinds.relevance: CAA, Heathrow, IAG, Menzies, Swissport, UPS, Aurrigo, Oxa and Fusion Processing formed a UK group to advance connected/automated airside mobility, lowering regulatory and adoption barriers for autonomous GSE at UK airports including STN

tailwinds.headline: Ryanair launches UK Winter 2025 schedule with 4 new Stansted routes (Lubeck, Munster, Murcia, Trapani)
tailwinds.relevance: Continued capacity growth by STN's dominant carrier adds turnarounds and baggage tow demand on a ramp run by a single dedicated handler

operationsNotes: STN is Ryanair's largest base worldwide; Ryanair added 4 new Stansted routes for winter 2025 and is growing its London-based fleet. Ramp is handler-led: Ryanair outsources all ground ops to Blue Handling (OmniServ/ABM), Swissport handles other carriers plus cargo, Menzies and dnata run cargo facilities (~75,000 tonnes/yr). The 2018/19 handler switch was triggered by baggage-delay failures, showing sensitivity to ramp performance. Expansion construction beginning 2025 will add terminal capacity by a third and a 14.3 MW solar farm.
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE vendor found on the STN ramp. Attractive profile: single dominant LCC (Ryanair, ~1,000-person dedicated Blue Handling operation, high-frequency narrowbody turns), a £1.1bn expansion lifting capacity from ~30M to 43M passengers, active airport labor disputes, and a new UK Airside Automation Group (CAA, Swissport, Menzies, Aurrigo, Oxa) legitimizing airside autonomy in the UK. Retrofit fit is strong given large existing baggage tractor fleets; note Aurrigo and Oxa are UK-local competitors with growing UK institutional ties.

name: Jeju International Airport

groundHandlers.name: Korea Airport Service (KAS, Hanjin Group)
groundHandlers.role: Ramp, baggage loading/sorting, cargo handling; Jeju branch listed; primary handler for Korean Air and Jin Air
groundHandlers.notes: Korean Air states it provides cargo handling at CJU via its handling arm; KAS lists a Jeju branch. Sources: https://cargo.koreanair.com/en/services/Ground-Handling-Service and https://www.kas.co.kr/kas/location

groundHandlers.name: Sharp Aviation K
groundHandlers.role: Ramp, cargo, passenger handling and GSSA for foreign carriers at 8 Korean airports including Jeju
groundHandlers.notes: Source: https://eng.sharp.co.kr/en/sub/company/overview.asp

groundHandlers.name: Jeju Air Service (JAS, ex Dongbo Airport Services)
groundHandlers.role: Ground agent for multiple domestic and international carriers; Jeju Air affiliated
groundHandlers.notes: Operation at CJU specifically not confirmed. Source: https://centreforaviation.com/data/profiles/suppliers/jeju-air-service

tailwinds.headline: MOLIT announces basic plan for New Jeju Airport (KRW 5.5T, 16.9M pax, 28 stands)
tailwinds.relevance: Capacity expansion locks in long-term Jeju traffic growth and creates a future greenfield ramp where autonomous GSE could be specified from day one

tailwinds.headline: Jeju deploys RideFlux Tamra autonomous vehicles on route serving Jeju International Airport
tailwinds.relevance: Island-wide regulatory and public acceptance of autonomous driving, including an airport corridor route, lowers approval friction for airside autonomy pilots

tailwinds.headline: Korea Airports Corporation wins 2025 Policy Expo grand prize for AI smart airport program
tailwinds.relevance: The operator of CJU is actively funding AI and automation across its 14 airports, a natural sponsor channel for autonomous GSE trials

operationsNotes: Handling at CJU is performed by handling companies rather than carriers directly, but the largest (KAS) is a Hanjin/Korean Air affiliate and JAS is Jeju Air affiliated, so commercial decisions track airline groups. Nov 2025: roughly 5,200 workers at three KAC subsidiaries struck across all 14 KAC airports including Jeju, citing near-minimum wages (avg KRW 2.48M/month) and understaffing; strikers were KAC subsidiary service/security staff rather than ramp handlers, but it signals broad airport labor strain (https://www.hankookilbo.com/News/Read/A2025111112330003680).
aerovectNotes: No autonomous GSE vendor found on the CJU ramp (TractEasy, Aurrigo, UISEE searches returned no Korea deployments). CJU anchors the world's busiest domestic route (GMP-CJU) with high-frequency narrowbody turns, acute airport labor cost pressure, and an automation-minded operator (KAC). Handlers are Korean airline affiliates, so entry paths are KAS/Korean Air, Sharp Aviation K, or a KAC smart-airport pilot. New Jeju Airport (early 2030s) adds a greenfield angle.

name: Salt Lake City International Airport

groundHandlers.name: Delta Air Lines (self-handling)
groundHandlers.role: Hub carrier, in-house ramp and bag handling
groundHandlers.notes: Delta owns and operates roughly 90% of all GSE at SLC per Airport Improvement (Nov 2024): https://airportimprovement.com/article/salt-lake-city-intl-enhances-airside-sustainability-with-electric-ground-support-equipment/ . SLC also returned to 100% Delta mainline gate and ramp agents per FlyerTalk discussion: https://www.flyertalk.com/forum/delta-air-lines-skymiles/2137801-how-does-delta-choose-stations-get-mainline-ground-staff.html

groundHandlers.name: Unifi Aviation
groundHandlers.role: Third-party ramp handling for non-Delta carriers
groundHandlers.notes: Active SLC ramp agent posting for Frontier (F9) handling: https://www.ziprecruiter.com/c/Unifi-Aviation-LLC/Job/Airport-Ramp-Agent-(F9)-SLC/-in-Salt-Lake-City,UT?jid=e90a56cb764f8ba7 . Unifi is ex-Delta Global Services with Delta holding a minority stake.

groundHandlers.name: Menzies Aviation
groundHandlers.notes: Menzies lists an SLC station providing into-plane fuel services: https://menziesaviation.com/our-network/salt-lake-city-slc/

groundHandlers.name: GAT Airline Ground Support (now PrimeFlight)
groundHandlers.role: Ground handling presence indicated (AeroVect partner network)
groundHandlers.notes: GAT shows Salt Lake City, UT job listings suggesting an SLC station: https://www.indeed.com/cmp/Gat-Airline-Ground-Support/locations/UT/Salt%20Lake%20City . Scope of SLC services unverified. Signature, G2 Secure Staff, and Quantem also hire ramp staff per the airport jobs page: https://slcairport.com/business-services/employment/job-opportunities-at-slc-international/

tailwinds.headline: SLC hits 96% electric GSE conversion with 442 chargers installed and 94 more planned
tailwinds.relevance: A fully electrified, airport-charged GSE fleet (90% Delta-owned) is the ideal substrate for electric autonomous tractor retrofits; capital and charging infrastructure are already in place.
